title: Merch Checking to see if an End of Evangelion DVD is legit
display_title: Merch Checking to see if an End of Evangelion DVD is legit
wikitext:
[]: [Merch] Checking to see if an End of Evangelion DVD is legit             Quote Post by notrogersmith » Tue Mar 04, 2014 9:18 pm Rather belatedly, I started to look for a copy of End of Evangelion on eBay, and found what I think is a legit used DVD, but I'm still a bit paranoid. The case of the DVD looks fine: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Tue Mar 04, 2014 10:01 pm Looks like the one I have, and it came with all the official stuff so I'd say that's legit.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 04, 2014 10:48 pm It's legit. The Death & Rebirth DVD was a double sided release with a special interactive encyclopedia thing on the second side. To be consistent, EoE looks like a double sided disc even though it isn't.

[]:             Quote Post by notrogersmith » Wed Mar 05, 2014 6:57 am Thanks. I went ahead and bought it. It'll be ... interesting ... to see EoE again. Been a while since I watched it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 05, 2014 8:16 am PRO TIP: If you're thinking about giving it a watch with audio commentary, ignore the commentary on the disc and watch it with this instead: http://www.evamonkey.com/end-of-evangelion-audio-commentary-with-rocco-and-garrett-of-mega64/

[]:             Quote Post by astrophys » Sat Mar 08, 2014 5:03 am Well, seeing that the consensus is that yours is legit, you're luckier than I was. That is to say, I myself got duped. Got End of Eva from Ebay myself. I wasn't as savvy about DVDs as I was now, so at the time I sincerely thought that I was buying something legitimate. (Now that I'm more savvy, I realize in retrospect that there were actually warning signs that I should have picked up upon, but I didn't recognize those at the time). Didn't realize my mistake until long later when I was looking at the Evageeks forums (as an unregistered/guest viewer) and came across another topic like this one. That's when I saw a version like the one that I had get discussed and pointed out as fake. Also, my discs now are behaving more temperamentally than they used to; they play perfectly fine in some disc drives but not others now. Really, the only bright side for me out of this whole scenario is that I got it at a much cheaper price than End of Eva typically goes for, but as the saying goes, "You get what you pay for."

[]:             Quote Post by notrogersmith » Sat Mar 08, 2014 4:01 pm astrophys wrote: Well, seeing that the consensus is that yours is legit, you're luckier than I was.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Mar 08, 2014 5:25 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: PRO TIP: If you're thinking about giving it a watch with audio commentary, ignore the commentary on the disc and watch it with this instead: http://www.evamonkey.com/end-of-evangelion-audio-commentary-with-rocco-and-garrett-of-mega64/

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sat Mar 08, 2014 5:32 pm Have not watched any of the English commentary on any of my Eva disks and that feature isn't a selling point for me.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Mar 08, 2014 7:30 pm Honestly, I wouldn't bother with the official release. It's out of print, and the quality was atrocious. It wasn't even anamorphic widescreen for fuck's sake.
